task_completion.description: Verify that the Slicer layout has been changed to a multi-view configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The central workspace shows a split-screen layout (e.g., Side-by-Side, Compare, or Four-Up) rather than a single large slice view.

task_completion.description: Confirm that at least two slice views are clearly visible for comparison.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least two distinct 2D slice panels (often with colored borders like Red/Yellow or labeled Compare 1/2) are visible.

task_completion.description: Check that two different MRI sequences are assigned to the comparison views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The volume names in the slice controller headers (top of each view) differ (e.g., one shows '..._t1ce' and the other '..._flair').

task_completion.description: Verify that view linking is enabled in the slice controllers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chain-link icon in the slice controller toolbar is highlighted, depressed, or active.

task_completion.description: Confirm that the linked views are displaying the same anatomical slice location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice offset numbers (e.g., 'Axial: 25.00 mm') displayed in the corners or headers of the views are identical.

task_completion.description: Verify that the final configuration was saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window is visible showing 'linked_views.png' in the 'Documents/SlicerData/Exports' folder, or the Save Screenshot dialog is shown with this filename.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent is interacting with the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot shows the full 3D Slicer interface (module panel, toolbar, menu bar), not an external image viewer or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GUI rather than purely programmatic commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual cues of GUI interaction such as open dropdown menus, highlighted buttons, or the mouse cursor over UI elements.

integrity.description: Confirm that actual MRI data is being rendered.
integrity.visual_evidence: The slice views display grayscale brain MRI anatomy, not blank screens, noise, or error messages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains MRI volumes with suffixes '_flair', '_t1', '_t1ce', and '_t2'. Valid multi-view layouts include 'Compare', 'Side by Side', 'Four-Up', and 'Conventional'. The slice views typically have colored borders (Red, Yellow, Green) or are labeled 'Compare 1' and 'Compare 2'. The link icon is a small chain symbol in the bar above the image.
